column4: Pod is killed when it exceeds memory limits
column5: Increase memory limits in pod spec or optimize application to use less memory
column6: kubectl get pods -o jsonpath='{.items[*].status.containerStatuses[*].lastState.terminated.reason}' | kubectl describe pod <pod-name> | kubectl set resources deployment <name> --limits=memory=512Mi

column4: Container crashes and Kubernetes keeps restarting it
column5: Check application logs for errors, fix the code/config, or increase startup time with initialDelaySeconds
column6: kubectl logs <pod-name> | kubectl logs <pod-name> --previous | kubectl describe pod <pod-name>

column4: Kubernetes cannot pull the container image from registry
column5: Verify image name, tag, registry credentials, or ensure registry is accessible
column6: kubectl describe pod <pod-name> | kubectl create secret docker-registry <secret-name> --docker-server=<registry> | kubectl set serviceaccount deployment <name> <service-account>

column4: Pod cannot be scheduled on any node
column5: Check node resources, labels/selectors, PVC availability, or resource requests
column6: kubectl describe pod <pod-name> | kubectl top nodes | kubectl get events --sort-by='.lastTimestamp'

column4: Init container failed during startup sequence
column5: Check init container logs to identify the failure, fix configuration, or dependencies
column6: kubectl logs <pod-name> -c <init-container-name> | kubectl describe pod <pod-name> | grep Init | kubectl logs <pod-name> --previous

column4: Pod is forcibly removed due to node resource pressure
column5: Increase node resources, optimize pod resource usage, or add more nodes
column6: kubectl describe pod <pod-name> | grep Reason | kubectl get events | kubectl top pod <pod-name>

column4: Container fails repeatedly and Kubernetes increases wait time between restarts
column5: Check application logs, fix startup issues, or increase startupProbe initialDelaySeconds
column6: kubectl logs <pod-name> | kubectl logs <pod-name> --tail=50 | kubectl describe pod <pod-name>

column4: Generic container error - application exited with non-zero status
column5: Review application logs, check exit code, verify environment variables and configuration
column6: kubectl logs <pod-name> | kubectl describe pod <pod-name> | kubectl exec -it <pod-name> -- /bin/sh

column4: Pod CPU usage exceeds limits and is being throttled
column5: Increase CPU limits or optimize application performance
column6: kubectl top pod <pod-name> | kubectl set resources deployment <name> --limits=cpu=1000m | kubectl describe pod <pod-name>

column4: Application cannot connect to service or external resource
column5: Verify service exists, DNS resolution works, firewall rules, or network policies
column6: kubectl get svc | kubectl get networkpolicies | kubectl exec -it <pod-name> -- nslookup <service-name> | kubectl logs <pod-name>
